description: Queue of section expansion tasks for the analyst. Each item references a curmudgeon review that found major weaknesses. The analyst writes complete replacement text, one item per run.

items.target: FOUNDATIONAL: New top-level Scoreboard tab + per-row scoreboard schema extensions (data/wins.json, data/predictions.json, new data/killshots.json). Phase 1 = 15 rows.
items.notes: FOUNDATIONAL MODEL CHANGE per HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001. Analyst draft produced 2026-04-20. Decider must (a) route to curmudgeon with REQUIRE_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW, (b) NOT auto-integrate, (c) create tracking issue, (d) log chg-*.json per HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001. Hold is in effect until operator posts HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001. The curmudgeon review is for HUMAN-GROUP CONSUMPTION only; it does NOT by itself authorise integration.
items.completion_notes: Phase 1 draft complete: 15 rows, schema spec, scoreboard tab HTML + intro paragraph copy, two formula-script skeletons (V13 Finsler Sydney-Perth + θ=−lonE) with unit tests, integration-point plan, eight anticipated objections. Status locked to draft_awaiting_human_group_review; HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 kept status=pending per companion routing HNOTE explicit instruction.

items.target: WIN-036 OTS cross-page consistency — Timestamp Error tab vs OTS WIN prose
items.notes: Two conflicting accounts of what status_history.json contains. Timestamp Error tab (generate-html.js ~line 1832) says prediction parameters live ONLY in monitor.py/model.html. OTS WIN prose (WINs 031/035/036/037/038/039) says status_history.json bundles prediction-side fields alongside observation fields. Option A: update tab prose to acknowledge mixed structure while maintaining separate-document critique (requires build-scripts/generate-html.js edit, PROP-016 stranded path). Option B: revert OTS WIN prose to simpler language consistent with tab (wins.json self-apply). Evaluate which is more defensible and propose the fix. If choosing Option A, produce exact replacement prose for the ~3 sentences starting at "OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json" in generate-html.js.
items.completion_notes: Mode 1 / Option A. Three exact-replacement patches against build-scripts/generate-html.js (Timestamp Error tab scorecard + main paragraph + WIN-035-039 bullet). Reuses EXP-290 P6/P7/P8 audit-grounded replacement strings; re-verified count=1 each on current origin/main HEAD. Decider self-apply expected. Run tests/run.sh after build per CLAUDE.md.
items.stranded_notes: EXP-294 patches target build-scripts/generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH per PROP-016). Decider created stranded-patches-2026-05-08T09-18-09Z.json for operator manual application. ISS-1875 set to pending-human. EXP-294 remains un-integrated until operator applies and confirms.
items.integration_note: Operator-required: patches target build-scripts/generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH). Stranded for operator manual apply.
items.integrated_by: decider (patches verified applied: grep confirms old find-strings absent, replacement text present in build-scripts/generate-html.js at HEAD; ISS-1875 closed by commit cb8a27eb)

items.notes: defense_survives=4 pivot: dome specified 3:1 and named mechanism (aetheric sub-terrestrial return path). Analyst must add: (a) pre-emption that 3:1 is adopted from CHAOS-7, not derived from disc_radius/firmament_height; (b) SAA-shoulder mechanism is post-hoc (dome geometry has no torque mechanism that specifically selects the SAA latitude over other longitudes).
items.completion_notes: Mode 3 / WIN-063 defense neutralization. Three find/replace patches against data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence: (P1) extend parameter list to all 4 dome geometric parameters; (P2) add SAA-longitudinal-arbitrariness paragraph (mirror-image ~150°E counterfactual against axisymmetric H(r)); (P3) replace buried "(Note: ...)" parenthetical with a labeled defender-anticipation paragraph addressing "you accept Olson & Deguen as fitting too" rebuttal — independent-input MHD via PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography heat-flux maps vs no-input ratio-naming. detail_verdict_text and TLDRs already updated by prior decider integration; no edits there. Post-patch detail_evidence length: 5,672 chars (was 3,678; +54%). NOTE: file naming collision — tracker EXP-295 was re-keyed at 2026-05-09T09:30Z to point at this WIN-063 defense; the path monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295.json is occupied by an earlier WIN-060/ISS-1866 expansion (2026-05-09T08:10Z, complete/integrated). This expansion is at -WIN-063-defense.json suffix; decider should consult JSON body item_id, not filename.

items.notes: defense_survives=3 pivot: dome concedes magnitude wrong but claims direction-match (NH>SH) as meaningful. Analyst must add sentence: NH>SH direction is independently predicted by mainstream energy balance (land mass / albedo asymmetry), so direction-match does not discriminate dome from standard science.
items.completion_note: Single-patch defense neutralization for ISS-1917 (WIN-066 c3 defense_survives=3). Find/replace in detail_verdict_text expanding the existing sign-non-discrimination sentence into a labeled three-counter Defender pivot anticipated paragraph that addresses both sign-match and magnitude-disclaim prongs. Filename = EXP-296-WIN-066-defense.json (not the 1KB EXP-296.json stub from 09:17Z which mistargeted WIN-043).
items.integration_note: Patches applied in commit a94caa73 (push 403; workspace-sync rescue queued)

items.notes: defense_survives=3 pivot: (a) 15.6% deviation needs anchoring to a specific dome tolerance; (b) 180° ambiguity — short arc 152° vs full arc 208°. Analyst must: (a) reference a specific dome WIN that implies a tolerance; (b) clarify that 152° is the relevant arc (both anomalies on same side of planet).

defense_survives=3 pivot: dome narrows claim to "pole region" (not all southern stars). Counter: (a) Canopus is at δ=-52°, well within "pole-adjacent" territory by any reasonable definition; (b) if dome energy falls off toward the rim, then all high-southern-declination stars should be dimmer — Canopus falsifies this. Analyst should add the Canopus δ=-52° fact and note the pole-region defense proves too much.
